title: ShopPlugin
description: A all in one shop plugin for your needs
body:
# ShopPlugin 

## Overview

ShopPlugin is a powerful, feature-rich economy plugin for Spigot/Bukkit servers that provides an intuitive GUI-based shopping experience. This plugin allows server administrators to create organized, multi-section shops with custom items, prices, and visual theming. Players can easily browse and purchase items through an interactive menu system, with full support for buying and selling transactions powered by Vault economy integration.

The plugin features a sleek, customizable double-chest transaction menu that lets players adjust quantities, toggle between buying and selling modes, and confirm purchases with a single click. With automatic inventory management, price calculations, and comprehensive configuration options, ShopPlugin transforms your server's economy into an engaging and user-friendly system.

## Key Features

- **Multi-Section Shop System** - Organize items into custom shop sections for better navigation
- **Dual Transaction Mode** - Toggle between buying and selling items with visual feedback
- **Customizable GUI** - Fully themed menus with support for glass pane borders, custom materials, and color-coded displays
- **Economy Integration** - Primary support for Vault with optional Essentials compatibility
- **Dynamic Pricing** - Set individual buy and sell prices for each item
- **Inventory Management** - Automatic space checking and item stack handling
- **Bulk Selling** - Sell all items of a type at once with the `/sellall` command
- **Real-time Configuration** - Reload shop configurations without server restart

## Commands

| Command | Permission | Description |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| `/shop` | `shop.open` | Opens the main shop menu with all available sections |
| `/shopreload` | `shop.reload` | Reloads all shop and section configurations |
| `/sellall` | `shop.sellall` | Automatically sells all items in inventory that are in the shop |

## Customization Features

### Transaction Menu Configuration
- **Menu Size** - Adjustable inventory size (supports single and double chests)
- **Button Positioning** - Customize slot positions for all interactive buttons
- **Border Styling** - Control glass pane border display and materials
- **Color Themes** - Change active/inactive button colors and glass pane data values
- **Display Names** - Fully customizable button names with color code support
- **Price Display** - Toggle price information visibility on the quantity display

### Shop Item Configuration
- **Material Selection** - Any Minecraft material for shop items
- **Custom Names** - Display names with color codes for visual appeal
- **Quantity Per Transaction** - Set default amounts for each item type
- **Individual Pricing** - Unique buy/sell prices for each item
- **Lore Support** - Add descriptive text to item tooltips

### Global Settings
- **Back Button** - Customize material, name, position, and global slot enforcement
- **Border Styling** - Full border customization for section menus
- **Economy Provider** - Choose between Vault or Essentials
- **File Organization** - Automatic categorization into Shops and Sections folders

### Button Customization
Each button in the transaction menu can be individually configured:
- **Buy Button** - Active/inactive materials and colors with toggle lore
- **Sell Button** - Separate active/inactive styling for transaction mode
- **Quantity Adjuster** - Increment/decrement buttons with custom materials
- **Confirm Button** - Visual confirmation element with custom styling
- **Cancel Button** - Styled exit button with configurable appearance


## Permissions

- `shop.open` - Allows players to open and browse the shop
- `shop.reload` - Allows reloading of all configurations
- `shop.sellall` - Allows using the bulk sell command

## Installation

1. Place the JAR file in your server's `plugins` folder
2. Ensure Vault is installed and an economy provider is active
3. Restart the server
4. Customize configurations in the generated ShopPlugin folder
5. Use `/shopreload` to apply changes without restarting
